session.name: MPI-8 Linux portable engine install brought end-to-end on a no-GPU laptop

goal.original: Continue MPI-8. Make the Linux portable build actually install ComfyUI and run, validated on the user's real (GPU-less, old) Ubuntu laptop.
goal.summary: Linux engine install now completes end-to-end (git auto-provision + comfy-cli layout + pip-seeded uv venv + CPU launch). The install succeeds and ComfyUI launches in CPU mode. Two open bugs remain: a version-stamp ReferenceError after install (retry works) and a no-models project-page UX bug (side-drawer flicker). Branch is pushed but NOT merged, pending these fixes + user validation of a generation.

completed: Fixed broken Linux tarball: hand-rolled tar writer dropped symlinks + exec bits (exit 18 'NPM not found'). Launchers now call electron/dist/electron directly + self-chmod; isExecutableEntry() marks binary/scripts/uv/.bin 755. (commit on master, merged)

Set D:\CubricStudio\Vision\Builds as the canonical build-distribution folder; local Windows builds default --stage-dir there. Documented build process in docs/releases/portable-distribution-contract.md + CLAUDE.md router. (merged to master)

Git auto-provision for Linux/mac engine install (routes/gitProvision.js): use host git else install via pkexec/brew; GIT_PYTHON_GIT_EXECUTABLE + GIT_PYTHON_REFRESH=quiet. VALIDATED on laptop (pkexec dialog appeared, git installed).

Fixed Linux/mac engine layout to match comfy-cli: <engine>/ComfyUI_linux IS the repo root (no /ComfyUI subdir), uv venv moved to sibling <engine>/comfy-venv, VIRTUAL_ENV reuse, stale-workspace guard. getComfyRepoRel() single source, sent to client. VALIDATED (clone succeeded).
Seeded uv venv with pip (uv venv --seed) so comfy-cli DependencyCompiler's 'python -m pip install pip uv' works. VALIDATED (got past it).
Guarded logger console writes against EIO on app close (routes/logger.js) — was throwing 'JavaScript error in main process' dialog. VALIDATED (no dialog).

Launch ComfyUI in CPU mode when no GPU detected (routes/comfy.js): mirrors the install vendor; --cpu (drop --lowvram) when no vendor. comfy.js is the only runtime launch path; run_nvidia_gpu.bat is patched but never executed. VALIDATED (install completed, custom nodes installed, ComfyUI launched).
pending: FIX BUG 1: routes/engine.js:351 'ReferenceError: engineInfo is not defined'. engineInfo is only defined in the Windows branch (_runEngineDownload else-block ~line 261-265); the Linux/uv path never defines it, so the post-install version-stamp step crashes. Use config.engine.version (config is in scope at ~line 240) or COMFY_VERSION instead of engineInfo.version. Retry currently works because the engine is already installed, but the stamp still fails.

FIX BUG 2 (no-models project UX): When a user creates/opens a project with no models installed, the model side-drawer keeps opening and closing (flicker loop), forcing the user back to the projects page to open the models folder. Requested fix: (a) remove the open/close flicker loop, (b) show a popup when entering a project with zero models installed, prompting the user to go back to the projects page and install a model.
After fixes: rebuild via CI on branch mpi-8/git-auto-provision, user re-validates on laptop, then run a real (slow CPU) generation test.
When validated: merge mpi-8/git-auto-provision -> master (git auto-provision + layout + pip-seed + logger guard + CPU launch). Branch currently NOT merged.
Deferred (separate task): GPU policy / gating for no-GPU users (warn vs block). User explicitly deferred this; not a blocker now.

task_card.title: Portable distribution, updater, and release validation

completed: Saved memory project_portable_tar_exec_symlink.md (tar drops symlinks/exec bits; launcher calls electron binary directly) + indexed in MEMORY.md.
Refined memory project_per_os_ci_build.md (workflow_dispatch works on any branch when YAML on default branch; gh run download into Builds; verify exec bit).
Documented build process in docs/releases/portable-distribution-contract.md (Build Process + Host Dependencies sections) + Linux/mac README templates + CLAUDE.md router entry. All merged to master.
pending: Consider a memory note for the comfy-cli engine layout (workspace IS repo root on Linux/mac, venv is sibling comfy-venv, VIRTUAL_ENV reuse, uv venv --seed needed for pip) once the path is fully validated with a generation.
Update validation.md in the MPI-8 task workspace with the laptop validation evidence (git auto-install via pkexec, clone, CPU install completed, ComfyUI launched) before end-session.

next_action.description: Fix routes/engine.js:351 (engineInfo undefined on the Linux/uv path — use config.engine.version or COMFY_VERSION), then fix the no-models project-page side-drawer flicker + add a 'no models installed' popup prompting the user to install a model. Then rebuild on branch mpi-8/git-auto-provision via gh workflow, have the user re-validate on the laptop, and merge to master once a generation works.

key_decisions: Linux/mac engine install uses comfy-cli, which HARD-REQUIRES git. Chose install-or-use (pkexec/brew), NOT bundling a portable git (git is a tree of binaries+templates+CA certs, fragile to relocate per-OS; package manager does it correctly). pkexec chosen for elevation so it works on no-terminal launches.

Aligned our Linux layout to comfy-cli's reality rather than fighting it: comfy-cli clones ComfyUI directly INTO --workspace (must not pre-exist) and reuses an active VIRTUAL_ENV. So <engine>/ComfyUI_linux IS the repo root and the uv venv is a sibling (comfy-venv).
uv venvs are pip-less by default; comfy-cli's DependencyCompiler needs pip -> use 'uv venv --seed'.
Install vendor and launch mode MUST match. Install picks torch build by GPU vendor; launch now mirrors it (CPU install -> CPU launch). comfy.js is the single runtime launch path.
GPU gating for no-GPU users deferred by user; not added now.

Build/test loop: edit -> commit -> push branch -> gh workflow run build-portable.yml --ref mpi-8/git-auto-provision -f version=0.0.1 -> gh run download -n cubric-vision-linux-x64 -> move tar.gz to D:\CubricStudio\Vision\Builds (drop update zip) -> user tests on laptop. Each loop ~5-8 min CI.
constraints: Linux/mac engine changes CANNOT be tested on the Windows dev box — native node_modules + comfy-cli clone only run on real Linux. The user's old GPU-less Ubuntu laptop is the validation machine. Generation is very slow on CPU (torch build deps took 30+ min) — expected, not a bug.

Windows engine path (_provisionWindowsEngine, prebuilt 7z archive) is UNCHANGED and must stay so. All Linux/mac edits branch on USES_COMFY_CLI (linux||darwin). Windows path helpers verified byte-identical.
CI workflow_dispatch runs the master-registered build-portable.yml against --ref <branch>. Workflow file is on master; can dispatch any branch.
Never push the Docs sibling repo (hard rule in CLAUDE.md). Kanban writes are pre-authorized.
CAVEMAN MODE is active in this session (terse output). The next session may or may not have it.
files_modified: routes/engine.js (ensureGit, layout, --seed, vendor flag, BUG at line 351)
scripts/build-portable.mjs (exec bits + D: Builds default — merged to master)

resume_prompt: You are resuming MPI-8 (Portable distribution, updater, and release validation) on branch mpi-8/git-auto-provision. Read .agents/mpi-kanban/state/handoffs/c7a170ac-7972-4697-98b7-1b6b0f0c41cb.json and use mpi-continue. The Linux portable engine install was just brought end-to-end on the user's GPU-less Ubuntu laptop (git auto-provision via pkexec, comfy-cli layout fix, pip-seeded uv venv, logger EIO guard, CPU launch mode — all validated). Two bugs remain. First: routes/engine.js line 351 throws 'ReferenceError: engineInfo is not defined' — engineInfo is only defined in the Windows branch; the Linux/uv path must use config.engine.version (config is in scope ~line 240) or COMFY_VERSION for the version stamp. Second: opening a project with no models installed causes the model side-drawer to flicker open/close repeatedly; fix the flicker loop AND add a popup that prompts the user to go back to the projects page and install a model. Linux/mac engine code can ONLY be validated on the user's real Linux laptop (not the Windows dev box), and the build/test loop is: edit -> commit -> push -> gh workflow run build-portable.yml --ref mpi-8/git-auto-provision -f version=0.0.1 -> gh run download -n cubric-vision-linux-x64 -> move tar.gz into D:\CubricStudio\Vision\Builds -> user tests. Keep the Windows engine path (prebuilt archive) untouched; all Linux/mac branching is on USES_COMFY_CLI. The branch is pushed but NOT merged — merge to master only after the user validates a generation.
